aws.name: All IAM users uniquely identified
aws.expected: All IAM users have unique usernames mapping to individual personnel
aws.remediation: Ensure all IAM users are uniquely named and map to individual personnel.

aws.name: Service accounts have descriptive names
aws.expected: Service roles/accounts are clearly identifiable with purpose descriptions
aws.remediation: Name service accounts descriptively and add tags identifying their purpose.

aws.name: EC2 instances use instance profiles
aws.expected: All EC2 instances use IAM instance profiles instead of embedded credentials
aws.remediation: Attach IAM instance profiles to EC2 instances; remove embedded credentials.

azure.name: All Azure AD users uniquely identified
azure.expected: All users have unique UPNs mapping to individual personnel
azure.remediation: Ensure all Azure AD accounts are uniquely identified to individuals.

azure.name: Managed identities used for service authentication
azure.expected: VMs and services use managed identities instead of service principal secrets
azure.remediation: Enable managed identities for Azure resources instead of using secrets.

gcp.name: All users identified via Google Cloud Identity
gcp.expected: All user bindings use organizational Google accounts, not personal gmail
gcp.remediation: Ensure all IAM bindings use organizational Cloud Identity accounts.

gcp.name: Service accounts clearly identified
gcp.expected: All service accounts have descriptive display names and descriptions
gcp.remediation: Add descriptive names and descriptions to all service accounts.

aws.name: Root account has MFA enabled
aws.remediation: Enable hardware MFA on the root account immediately.

aws.name: All IAM users with console access have MFA
aws.expected: All users with password_enabled=true have mfa_active=true
aws.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users with console access.

azure.name: MFA registration required for all users
azure.remediation: Require MFA registration for all Azure AD users.

azure.name: Legacy authentication blocked
azure.expected: Conditional Access policy blocks legacy authentication protocols
azure.remediation: Create Conditional Access policy to block legacy authentication.

gcp.name: 2-Step Verification enforced
gcp.expected: 2-Step Verification enforced for all users in the organization
gcp.remediation: Enforce 2-Step Verification in Google Workspace admin console.

aws.name: MFA enforced for all console access
aws.expected: 100% of console users have MFA enabled
aws.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users with console access.

aws.name: MFA required for privileged API actions
aws.expected: IAM policies require MFA (aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent) for sensitive actions
aws.remediation: Add MFA condition to IAM policies for privileged actions.

aws.name: Hardware MFA used for root account
aws.expected: Root account uses hardware MFA token (not virtual)
aws.remediation: Replace virtual MFA with hardware MFA token for root account.

azure.name: MFA required via Conditional Access
azure.expected: Conditional Access policy requires MFA for all users on all cloud apps
azure.remediation: Create Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for all cloud applications.

azure.name: MFA required for Azure management
azure.expected: MFA required for Azure portal, CLI, and PowerShell access
azure.remediation: Configure Conditional Access to require MFA for Azure management.

gcp.name: 2-Step Verification enforced organization-wide
gcp.expected: 2-Step Verification enforced for all users, not just encouraged
gcp.remediation: Set 2-Step Verification to 'Enforced' in Google Workspace admin console.

gcp.name: Security key required for admin accounts
gcp.expected: Super admin accounts require security key for 2SV
gcp.remediation: Require security keys for all super admin accounts.

aws.name: FIDO2/WebAuthn supported for MFA
aws.expected: FIDO2 security keys supported and preferred for MFA
aws.remediation: Support FIDO2 security keys as MFA devices for replay resistance.

aws.name: STS tokens are time-limited
aws.expected: All role session durations are appropriately limited
aws.remediation: Configure STS token durations to minimize replay attack window.

azure.name: FIDO2 authentication method enabled
azure.expected: FIDO2 security key authentication method enabled
azure.remediation: Enable FIDO2 security key authentication method in Azure AD.

gcp.name: Security key enforcement available
gcp.expected: Security key 2SV option available for all users
gcp.remediation: Enable security key support for replay-resistant authentication.

aws.name: Deleted IAM user names not reused within 90 days
aws.expected: Organizational process prevents reuse of IAM user names within defined period
aws.remediation: Implement naming convention that prevents identifier reuse (e.g., include date suffix).

azure.name: Soft-deleted user accounts not reused
azure.expected: Deleted user UPNs are in soft-delete state preventing reuse for 30 days
azure.remediation: Azure AD retains deleted users for 30 days; extend organizational policy for 90 days.

gcp.name: User account identifiers not reused
gcp.expected: Suspended/deleted user accounts not reassigned within 90 days
gcp.remediation: Implement policy to prevent reuse of user identifiers within defined period.

aws.name: IAM users inactive for 90 days identified
aws.expected: No IAM users with last activity > 90 days ago
aws.remediation: Disable IAM users inactive for more than 90 days.

aws.name: Access keys inactive for 90 days identified
aws.expected: No active access keys unused for more than 90 days
aws.remediation: Deactivate access keys not used in the last 90 days.

azure.name: Inactive Azure AD accounts identified
azure.expected: No user accounts with last sign-in > 90 days ago
azure.remediation: Disable Azure AD accounts inactive for more than 90 days.

gcp.name: Inactive service account keys identified
gcp.expected: No service account keys unused for more than 90 days
gcp.remediation: Delete service account keys inactive for more than 90 days.

aws.name: IAM password policy enforces complexity
aws.remediation: Configure IAM password policy with minimum 14 characters and complexity requirements.

azure.name: Azure AD password protection enabled
azure.expected: Custom banned password lists enabled and enforced
azure.remediation: Enable Azure AD Password Protection with custom banned password list.

gcp.name: Password policy enforced in Workspace
gcp.expected: Minimum password length of 14 characters enforced with complexity requirements
gcp.remediation: Configure password policy in Google Workspace with minimum 14 characters.

aws.name: Password reuse prevention configured
aws.remediation: Set IAM password policy to remember at least 24 previous passwords.

azure.name: Password history enforced
azure.expected: Password history enforcement prevents reuse of recent passwords
azure.remediation: Azure AD enforces password history by default; verify no custom policy disables it.

gcp.name: Password reuse restricted in Workspace
gcp.expected: Password reuse prevention enabled in Workspace admin settings
gcp.remediation: Configure password reuse prevention in Google Workspace admin.

aws.name: IAM Identity Center force password change on first login
aws.expected: New users are required to change password on first login
aws.remediation: Configure IAM Identity Center to require password change on first login.

azure.name: Force password change on new accounts
azure.remediation: Ensure all new user accounts require password change at first login.

gcp.name: Force password change for new users
gcp.expected: New users required to change password on first sign-in
gcp.remediation: Configure Google Workspace to require password change on first sign-in.

aws.name: TLS enforced for all API communication
aws.expected: All AWS API calls use TLS 1.2+ (enforced by AWS)
aws.remediation: AWS enforces TLS 1.2+ for API calls; verify client-side TLS configuration.

aws.name: Database passwords encrypted in transit
aws.expected: SSL/TLS enforcement enabled on all RDS instances
aws.remediation: Enable require_ssl parameter on all RDS instances.

azure.name: HTTPS-only access enforced
azure.remediation: Enable HTTPS Only on all Azure App Service web applications.

gcp.name: SSL enforced on Cloud SQL instances
gcp.expected: requireSsl=true on all Cloud SQL instances
gcp.remediation: Enable SSL enforcement on all Cloud SQL instances.

3.5.11.manual_guidance: Verify that authentication interfaces mask password input fields. Confirm that error messages do not reveal whether the username or password was incorrect (use generic messages like 'Invalid credentials'). Check web applications, API endpoints, and CLI tools.

objectives_requiring_documentation.text: authentication information is obscured during the authentication process.
objectives_requiring_documentation.evidence_needed: Provide documentation or process evidence: authentication information is obscured during the authentication process.
